facts.text: Vienna’s oldest university, founded in 1365 by Duke Rudolph IV, is the oldest and largest in the German‑speaking world.
The city’s Schönbrunn Palace park houses the world’s oldest operating zoo, founded in 1752.
Vienna’s name comes from the Roman castrum Vindobona, established in the 1st century on the Danube frontier.
The Ringstraße, a grand boulevard built in the late 19th century, surrounds Vienna’s historic centre and showcases Baroque palaces and monumental buildings.
The 1949 film The Third Man, set in post‑war Vienna, inspired the Mozarthaus Vienna Dritte Mann Museum dedicated to the movie.
Vienna’s English Theatre, founded in 1963 in Josefstadt, is the oldest English‑language theatre on continental Europe.
